name: CARE (Case Reports)
source: Gagnier JJ, Kienle G, Altman DG, Moher D, Sox H, Riley D; CARE Group. The CARE guidelines: consensus-based clinical case reporting guideline development. BMJ Case Rep 2013;2013:bcr2013201554. doi: 10.1136/bcr-2013-201554

items.text: The words 'case report' should be in the title along with the phenomenon of greatest interest (e.g. symptom, diagnosis, test, intervention).
2 to 5 key words capturing the key elements of the case.
Structured abstract: introduction, main symptoms and/or clinical findings, main diagnoses, therapeutic interventions and outcomes, conclusion.
One or two paragraphs summarising why this case is unique with references.
De-identified demographic information, main symptoms, medical/family/psycho-social history (including genetic, if relevant), relevant past interventions and outcomes.
Relevant physical examination and other clinical findings.
Historical and current information from this episode of care organised as a timeline.
Diagnostic methods; diagnostic challenges; diagnostic reasoning including other diagnoses considered; prognostic characteristics.
Types of intervention (pharmacologic, surgical, preventive, self-care); administration (dose, strength, duration, frequency); changes with rationale.
Clinician- and patient-assessed outcomes; important follow-up test results; adherence and tolerability; adverse and unanticipated events.
Strengths and limitations of your approach; discussion of relevant medical literature; rationale for conclusions (including assessments of cause and effect); main 'take-away' lessons.
The patient should share their perspective or experience whenever possible.
Statement of patient informed consent (or assent) to publish. If not provided, reasons why.
